action.reasoning: Observation: The predict.py script is failing because the model trained on h1hand-walk-v0 (151-dim obs) cannot handle the h1hand-package-v0 task (173-dim obs). The error message clearly states 'Unexpected observation shape (173,) for Box environment, please use (151,) or (n_env, 151) for the observation shape.' This confirms the issue mentioned in point #8 of the task description about obs_space mismatch. Analysis: The model trained on a single task with 151-dim observations cannot generalize to tasks with different observation dimensions (like h1hand-package-v0 with 173-dim obs). I need to implement a solution that handles variable observation dimensions. The two approaches mentioned are: (a) Train per-task models, or (b) Implement obs padding to a common dimension. Since I currently have only one model trained on walk, I need to either train models for each task separately or modify my approach to handle variable obs dimensions. Decision: I'll implement observation padding to a common dimension (max 173) and add a task identifier to the observation to help the model distinguish between tasks. This will allow me to use a single model for all tasks.
